body_text: Instead of single role, use multiple expert viewpoints collaborating
Example: "You are three experts: behavioral psychologist, copywriter, and data analyst"
Prevents generic single-role outputs
Creates richer, more nuanced responses
Define specific, measurable outcomes upfront
Instead of "make it good" -> "optimize for 40% open rate, 12% CTR, include 3 psychological triggers"
No metrics = no optimization
Gives AI clear targets to hit
Stack multiple context elements: business type, budget, audience, past performance
Example: "B2B SaaS, $200/mo product, targeting overworked founders, previous emails got 20% opens"
Context dramatically improves relevance
More detail = more targeted output
Break requests into sequential steps instead of one big ask
Example: "Step 1: Identify pain points. Step 2: Create hook. Step 3: Build value. Step 4: Soft CTA"
Prevents AI confusion and generic responses
Creates logical flow in output
Don't accept first output - build in self-critique
Example: "Rate your response 1-10 on clarity, persuasion, and actionability. Improve anything below 8"
Self-critique produces 10x better results
Iterative improvement built into single prompt
1. Single-role prompts = generic outputs - Always use multiple perspectives
2. No metrics = no optimization - Specify exact success criteria
3. Context is king - More background = better relevance
4. Break down tasks - Step-by-step prevents confusion
5. Force self-evaluation - AI critiquing itself improves quality dramatically
The D-E-P-T-H Prompt Framewok

visual_analysis.primary_purpose: To educate and provide a structured guide on how to write effective prompts for AI models using the 'D-E-P-T-H' framework.

components.description: Use multiple expert roles instead of a single one to get richer outputs.
Provide specific, measurable outcomes for the AI to target.
Give detailed background information (business type, audience, history) to improve relevance.
Break a large request into smaller, sequential steps to avoid confusion.
Instruct the AI to self-critique its output based on given criteria.

prompt_structure_and_components.summary_section: A 'TD;LR' (Too Long; Didn't Read) section summarizes the five key principles of the framework.

visual_analysis.formatting_and_organization: The document uses a clean, minimalist design with a clear visual hierarchy. A main title is at the top, followed by sections for each letter of the D-E-P-T-H acronym. The content is organized using bold section headers and bullet points for easy readability. The first four sections (D, E, P, T) are arranged in a two-column layout, while the H and TD;LR sections span the full width. A footer contains the document title and a page number.
